Insights – Manish Gunwani (CIO, Bandhan AMC)

Investment Process
• Focus on institutional research-driven process
• Coverage of 700+ small-cap companies
• Target coverage ~1000 companies

Stock Classification Framework
• L1: High-quality compounders
• L2: Mid-quality businesses with moderate predictability
• L3: Cyclical companies linked to economic cycles
• Portfolio balances quality + cyclicality

Multi-bagger Filter
• Key rule: Small Market Cap + Large TAM
• Example filter: ~₹20K Cr market cap with large industry opportunity

Market Valuation View
• Structural decline in inflation & interest rates
• Lower cost of equity supports higher PE multiples
• Small caps: 50–60% expensive but 30–40% still attractive

AI Trend
• Shift from AI infrastructure → AI applications
• Beneficiary sectors: Banking, Retail
• Underweight traditional IT services

Manufacturing Opportunity
• Drivers: China+1, currency advantage, supply chain diversification
• Strong outlook for engineering-led manufacturing growth

Sector Themes
• Real Estate: consolidation among top developers

• Auto ancillaries & chemicals: global market share gains

Long-Term Investment Themes
• Global Conquerors: Indian firms expanding globally
• Financialization of Savings: shift to capital markets
• Internet Platforms: scale + profitability driven models

ADANI PORTS ; PM Modi Inaugurates Adani Ports’ Fully Automated Haldia Bulk Terminal

• ⚓ New Port Facility: PM Modi inaugurates APSEZ’s Haldia Bulk Terminal, India’s first fully automated dry bulk terminal.

• 📦 Handling Capacity: Terminal designed to handle 4 MMTPA dry bulk cargo.

• 🚆 Integrated Logistics: Equipped with railway wagon loading system and dedicated rail line for ship-to-train cargo evacuation.

• 🏗️ Modern Infrastructure: Features automated conveyors, stacker-reclaimers, and mobile harbour cranes.

• 🌏 Strategic Location: Strengthens cargo movement for eastern India including West Bengal, Odisha, and Jharkhand.

🟢 Impact: Positive
Capacity addition and automation enhance Adani Ports’ efficiency, cargo handling capability, and logistics network.

IndiGo Adjusts Middle East Flight Operations Amid Geopolitical Tensions

• ✈️ Operational Adjustment: IndiGo to operate 252 weekly flights to/from Middle East between 16–28 March 2026.

• 🌍 Key Routes Active: 126 weekly flights to Saudi Arabia, 28 to Oman, 98 to UAE.

• ⚠️ Network Changes: Flights to Doha, Kuwait, Bahrain, Dammam, Fujairah, Ras Al Khaimah & Sharjah remain suspended till March 28.

• 🛢️ Reason: Adjustments due to geopolitical tensions, airspace restrictions, rising fuel & insurance costs.

🟡 Impact: Neutral to Slight Negative
Operational disruptions and route suspensions may impact traffic and costs in the short term.

• Slower Hindi belt expansion
• Competitive intensity
• Possible **GST regime changes**

Current State of Indian Markets (13-Mar-2026)

The table shows the % Fall of Indices (closing values) from their respective All-Time-Highs (intraday).

Eternal Bear Market Signal

Stock Rise Breakdown  
DIIs bought for 12 quarters, driving 8x gain from ₹45 (Mar 2023) to ₹368 peak—pure P/E expansion with negative earnings. Bear phase now triggers P/E compression.  

Holdings Evolution  
Mar 2023: DII 8%, FII 54%; Dec 2025: DII 33%, FII 36%. FIIs held to ₹290 (Aug 2024), sold post-₹300 peak; DIIs (SBI, HDFC, ICICI, etc.) bought at 1,500 P/E.  

Valuation Reality  
No earnings, negative cash flows 5 years post-IPO; narrative pushed "lakh crore" fantasies. Formula: Price = Earnings × P/E; zero earnings = bubble reliance.  

Cycle Mechanics  
P/E expands slowly in bulls (stairs up); compresses rapidly in bears (elevator down) via fear and mean reversion. Eternal drop may end bear, usher new bull.  

Core Lesson  
DII peak buying highlights narrative risks; P/E reset flags bear cycle turn.

**INDIA EV AUTO: LEGACY OEMs DOMINATE UPTREND**

**Big Picture**  
EV upcycle intact across E-2W/3W/PV, but **legacy OEMs consolidating** (~80% share) as startups falter on service/capital/execution. FY26 volumes: E-2W >1.3 Mn (+15%), E-PV ~200k (+84%), E-3W +16% YTD.

**E-2W Leadership**  
TVS **28%**, Bajaj **23%**, Ather **18%**, Hero **11%** = **80% share**.  
TVS iQube peaks ~35k/mo; Bajaj Chetak C2501 <₹1L; Ather Rizta family pivot; Hero VIDA BaaS ₹45k + ₹0.96/km.  
**Ola collapse**: ~53k → ~4k/mo on service woes.

**E-PV Concentration**  
Tata **40%**, JSW MG **25-30%**, Mahindra **15%** = **85%**.  
Tata full-stack (Tiago to Harrier.ev, BaaS Punch); Mahindra BE6/XEV 9e scaling to 7-8k pm FY27; JSW Windsor >75%, MG 520 PHEV Oct'26.  
Maruti e-Vitara entry; Hyundai/BYD ~3-4%.

**E-3W Acceleration**  
Fastest growth; fleet-driven (passenger/e-comm).  
Bajaj overtakes Mahindra; TVS +1400% YTD low-base. Subsidy end Dec'25 but momentum holds.

**Company Spotlights**  
**TVS**: iQube + Orbiter; ~33% EV growth vs 15% industry.  
**Bajaj**: Chetak/GoGo; 2W/3W swapping.  
**Mahindra**: Treo/e-Alfa ~12% 3W EV share FY26; XEV 9S deliveries.  
**Tata Motors**: Punch/Harrier/Curvv ramps.

**Themes**  
Service networks/reliability win; TCO > subsidies; GST ICE cuts narrow gap but EVs hold on running costs (~₹0.2/km vs ₹2 petrol). BaaS widens access.

**Key Takeaway**  
Legacy scale (TVS/Bajaj/Tata/Mahindra) trumps startups—position for consolidation phase.

CHEMICALS: THE CYCLE IS TURNING – 4 HIGH-CONVICTION THEMES

The chemical sector is quietly signalling the start of a multi-year upcycle, not a 1–2 quarter trade.

Macro Setup: Why This Time Is Different  
- Geopolitical disruption in the Strait of Hormuz is tightening global supply and lifting chemical prices month after month.  
- From April 2026, China removes export tax rebates on multiple chemicals, effectively scrapping the artificial pricing subsidy that kept global realizations suppressed.  
- Combine a crude-linked supply shock with removal of Chinese subsidies and you get the conditions for a structural repricing of Indian chemicals, not just a cyclical bounce.  
- Yet, many leading names are trading near 52-week lows, with valuations that assume the downcycle continues.

Below are 4 chemicals showing the sharpest price uptrends and the stocks best positioned if this cycle sustains.

1) Acetone – Deepak Nitrite  
- Price move: ~37% in 4 months (₹57 → ₹78/kg).  
- Stock: Deepak Nitrite | CMP ~₹1,490 | 52-week range: ₹1,460–₹2,174.  

Why it matters  
- Deepak Nitrite holds 50%+ domestic market share in Phenol and Acetone, making it the cleanest listed proxy to this move.  
- A new Dahej plant commissioned in Jan 2026 adds capacity right as the cycle turns up.  
- Removal of China’s export rebates further eases import pressure and supports realizations.  
- From today’s depressed earnings, even a modest improvement in spreads can translate into outsized EPS growth due to high operating leverage.  

Longer-term view  
If crude stays elevated and China maintains export discipline, Acetone prices can remain structurally above past averages for 6–10 quarters, off a very low earnings base today.

2) Aniline – Nocil  
- Price move: ~18% in 4 months (₹122 → ₹144/kg).  
- Stock: Nocil | CMP ~₹157 | 52-week range: ₹125–₹253.  

Why it matters  
- Stock is down ~40% in a year, trading near 1.3x P/B, as if its leadership in rubber chemicals is permanently impaired.  
- Aniline is a key input; its price firming up improves the pricing environment for downstream rubber chemicals, aiding margin normalization.  
- Tyre manufacturers are operating at healthy utilisation levels, offering steady volume visibility.  
- Anti-dumping petitions against Korean and Chinese rubber chemical imports are under review, with decisions expected in the coming months.  

Longer-term view  
If anti-dumping duties come through and Aniline prices stay firm, Nocil’s margin recovery could play out as a 2–3 year story the market is not yet discounting at current valuations.

3) VAM (Vinyl Acetate Monomer) – Aarti Industries  
- Price move: ~26% in 4 months (₹80 → ₹101/kg).  
- Stock: Aarti Industries | CMP ~₹433 | 52-week range: ₹344–₹495.  

Why it matters  
- After a harsh margin compression cycle, the stock is only ~12% lower over 1 year, but EBITDA margins have already bottomed and turned (from ~12.5% to ~13.9% YoY in Q3 FY26).  
- There is still significant runway to revert towards historical 20%+ margin zones if spreads keep normalising.  
- Exports form ~65% of revenue, and the India–US trade deal cutting tariffs on Indian chemical exports from 50% to 18% adds an external tailwind.  
- Aarti has ~₹1,100 Cr capex planned in FY26, positioning capacity ahead of the next demand wave.  

Longer-term view  
Global VAM capacity typically takes 3–4 years to come onstream, so current supply tightness can sustain elevated pricing into FY27 and beyond.

4) Heavy Soda Ash – GHCL  
- Price move: ~14% in 4 months (₹28 → ₹32/kg).  
- Stock: GHCL | CMP ~₹510 | 1-year return: -24%.  

Why it matters  
- GHCL trades around 9x P/E and 1.3x P/B after a 24% drawdown, pricing in a prolonged downcycle.  
- It is a pure-play Soda Ash business, so every rupee of price improvement flows directly into EBITDA without dilution from unrelated segments

reports.text:
Asia’s Hormuz Exposure Scorecard – Nomura

• Energy Security Risk: Asia is at the epicenter of potential energy supply shocks if disruptions at the Strait of Hormuz continue.

• Stagflation Risk: Prolonged disruption (>1 month) could trigger stagflation — higher inflation with slower growth.

• Most Exposed Economies:
  – Thailand
  – South Korea
  – India

• Key Reason: High dependence on Middle East oil and gas imports passing through the Strait of Hormuz.

Implication:  
Sustained disruption could lead to higher oil prices, rising inflation and slower economic growth across Asia.

reports.text:
West Asia Oil Flow Situation – Impact of Strait of Hormuz Disruption

The Persian Gulf (stretching from Kuwait & Iraq to the UAE) is the region where most Middle East oil and natural gas production is located.

The Strait of Hormuz connects the Persian Gulf to the Arabian Sea, making it the most critical oil shipping chokepoint in the world.  
Normally, oil tankers load crude in the Gulf and pass through Hormuz to reach global markets.

Alternate Pipelines Being Used

To reduce dependence on Hormuz, some Gulf countries built pipelines earlier.

• Saudi Petroline (East–West Pipeline):  
  Can transport ~5+ million barrels per day from the Gulf side of Saudi Arabia to the Red Sea, allowing ships to export oil to Europe, Africa and Asia without using Hormuz.

• Abu Dhabi Pipeline:  
  Can transport ~1.5 million barrels per day from the Gulf to the Arabian Sea.

Current Workaround

• Tankers stuck inside the Gulf (west of Hormuz) are transferring oil into Saudi and UAE pipelines.  
• Empty tankers outside Hormuz are sailing to Red Sea ports in Saudi Arabia or Oman to load oil transported through these pipelines.

This partially eases supply disruption, but the problem remains because Hormuz normally handles far more oil than these pipelines combined.

Iran’s Oil Strategy

• Iran continues exporting ~1 million+ barrels/day through Hormuz.  
• The US has not blocked Iranian exports because it does not want global oil prices to spike.  
• Iran, however, benefits from higher oil prices while still earning revenue from exports.

Production Impact

Some oil and gas plants in Qatar, Saudi Arabia and nearby countries have temporarily reduced output due to logistical disruptions.

To stabilise markets:

• IEA has released emergency oil stocks  
• US is releasing oil from its Strategic Petroleum Reserve

Implication for India

• India is unlikely to face major supply shortages.  
• Oil can still arrive via pipelines + alternate shipping routes.  
• India may also quietly increase purchases of Iranian crude if needed.

However, prices could remain elevated due to geopolitical risk.

Bigger Risk for India

If the conflict prolongs, the real concern may be gas, urea and helium supplies, which India imports significantly from the region.

India is already exploring alternative sourcing (e.g., Canada and other suppliers) to mitigate this risk.

West Asia Conflict Begins Impacting Exports from Telugu States

The ongoing West Asia conflict is starting to impact exports from Andhra Pradesh and Telangana, particularly in agriculture and MSME sectors.

• Fruit Exports Hit: Export orders for banana and mango from Gulf countries have been cancelled, forcing excess stock into domestic markets.

• MSME Export Challenges: Pharma, engineering and other MSME exporters are facing delays in shipments and higher freight costs.

Implication:  
Prolonged disruptions could pressure agri exporters, seafood exporters and MSME exporters in Andhra Pradesh and Telangana if the conflict continues.

reports.text:
NIFTY Insight – 25 Years of Monthly Data

Over the last 25 years, analysis of monthly NIFTY 50 data shows an interesting pattern between past returns and future performance.

• Only 17% of months saw past returns fall below 5%.

• In those cases, 77% of the time NIFTY delivered >13% returns over the next 1 year.  
  (≈ 13% is the long-term average annual return of NIFTY based on historical data.)

• Across all months, NIFTY has delivered more than 13% return after 1 year in about 48% of observations.

• Current Situation: NIFTY past return stands at ~6%, close to the lower-return zone historically associated with stronger forward returns.

Observation:  
Many of the periods when past returns were low were also times when pessimism about India or the global economy was very high.

Takeaway:  
Historically, lower past returns have often created better forward return probabilities for long-term investors.

Note: Definitions of “past return” and “future return” are provided in the reference image.

Iran Threat Puts Billions in US Tech Data Centres at Risk

Iran’s warning to target US technology infrastructure in the Gulf region has raised concerns about geopolitical risks to global digital infrastructure.

Data Centre Exposure:
• The Gulf region hosts 70+ data centres with ~557–738 MW capacity.  
• These facilities support major global cloud services and enterprise workloads.

Major Tech Presence:
• Key facilities operated by Amazon Web Services (AWS), Microsoft, Google, Oracle and Alibaba Cloud.

Recent Disruption:
• A drone attack on two AWS facilities recently disrupted services for banking apps and stock markets in the UAE.

Cloud Strategy Shift:
• Cloud providers are evaluating redirecting workloads from Gulf data centres to India and Singapore to mitigate geopolitical risks.

Global Business Impact:
• Any disruption could affect ERP systems, supply chains, financial platforms and analytics services used by multinational companies.

Strategic Importance of Gulf:
• The region is a major digital connectivity hub, carrying ~90% of Europe–Asia internet traffic via submarine cables and internet exchange points.

Implication for India:
• Indian companies relying on global cloud platforms for ERP, finance and analytics systems could face indirect disruptions if the situation escalates.

Outlook:

Power Stocks Rally Up to 8% on Surge in Electricity Demand

Shares of power generation and utility companies gained up to 8% amid expectations of strong electricity demand during the upcoming summer season.

Stock Moves:
• Adani Power, JSW Energy, BHEL, Tata Power and Torrent Power rose around 5–8%.  
• NTPC Green Energy surged nearly 15%, touching ₹99.75 during the session.

Sector Performance:
• The BSE Power Index jumped over 3% intraday to ~7,070, though still below its 52-week high of 7,143.

Demand Trend:
• India recorded peak power demand of ~224.6 GW at 7 PM on March 10, the highest-ever evening demand.

Power Supply Mix:
• Demand during non-solar hours was supported by wind, hydro, nuclear and coal-based generation.

Outlook:
• Analysts expect higher plant load factors for thermal power plants if summer temperatures remain high.  
• Lower rainfall and snow cover may lead to hydro generation shortfalls, increasing reliance on coal-based power.

Implication: Positive demand outlook for thermal and integrated power companies.

India Retail Inflation (CPI) – February Update

India’s retail inflation (CPI) rose to 3.21% in February, up from 2.74% in January, according to data released by the National Statistical Office (NSO).

Key Drivers:
• Food Inflation: Increased due to sharp price rises in coconut, tomato, cauliflower, and green chillies.  
• Food & Beverages Inflation: 3.35%.  
• Pan, Tobacco & Intoxicants: Inflation recorded at 3.49%.

Core Inflation:  
• Core inflation (excluding food & fuel) remained relatively stable at ~3.4%, indicating moderate underlying price pressures.

Rural vs Urban Inflation:
• Rural Inflation: 3.37%  
• Urban Inflation: 3.02%

State-wise Inflation:
• Highest: Telangana – 5.20%  
• Lowest: Mizoram – ~1.07%

Outlook:  
Economists warn that rising crude oil prices amid West Asia tensions could create upside risks to inflation in the coming months.

West Asia Conflict May Disrupt AC Production in India

Rising geopolitical tensions in West Asia and restrictions on commercial LPG supply are creating challenges for air conditioner (AC) manufacturers.

• LPG Usage: LPG is used in key manufacturing processes such as brazing and powder-coating, and supply constraints could impact production.

• Input Cost Pressure: Shortages and price increases in plastic inputs like polypropylene and polystyrene are adding further pressure.

• Commodity Inflation:  
  – Copper prices up ~25%  
  – Aluminium at record highs  
  Both are increasing overall AC manufacturing costs.

• Production Risk: Industry executives warn that AC production could be affected in the coming days if LPG supply restrictions persist.

• Seasonal Sensitivity: The disruption risk comes just ahead of the peak summer season, when AC demand typically surges.

• Industry Response: Companies are closely monitoring supply chains and adjusting procurement strategies to minimise disruptions.

Implication: Potential cost pressure and supply-side risks for AC manufacturers if the situation continues.

AI Boom Drives $700 Billion Surge in Data Center Lease Commitments  

- Microsoft and Meta Platforms have each committed nearly $50 billion in new data center leases in recent quarters to expand AI infrastructure.  

- These massive deals have pushed total future lease obligations of major cloud firms beyond $700 billion.  

- Other key players — including Amazon and Oracle — are also ramping up investments, fueling fierce competition in cloud and AI computing capacity.  

- Microsoft leads with about $155 billion in future lease commitments, followed by Meta at $104 billion.  

- Most leases cover data centers and server farms, though some include associated office and warehouse facilities.  

- The surge underscores the enormous capital intensity required for AI-driven data processing and cloud infrastructure.  

- Industry leaders caution that server farm and compute capacity shortages are emerging as major constraints amid the AI acceleration wave.  

Key Takeaway: The AI boom is triggering record data center leasing by tech giants, marking a new phase of infrastructure competition and capital expansion.

NSE Appoints 20 Merchant Bankers for Upcoming IPO  

- National Stock Exchange of India (NSE) has appointed 20 merchant bankers and eight law firms to manage its long-awaited initial public offering (IPO).  
- The appointed intermediaries will handle regulatory filings, due diligence, documentation, marketing, and execution of the issue.  
- Key merchant bankers include Morgan Stanley, JP Morgan, Citigroup, HSBC, Kotak Mahindra Capital, HDFC Bank, and Axis Capital.  
- In the unlisted market, NSE’s valuation stands at around ₹4.89 lakh crore, with shares trading near ₹1,975 per share.  
- The exchange recently received regulatory clearance after nearly a decade-long delay linked to the co-location case.  
- Last year, NSE made a ₹1,300 crore provision to settle related regulatory matters.  

Key Takeaway: With regulatory hurdles largely behind, NSE’s IPO — one of India’s most awaited listings — is finally moving closer to market debut.

MSMEs Likely to Increase Use of TReDS Financing Amid Trade Uncertainty  

- Rising geopolitical tensions in West Asia are expected to drive higher working capital demand through the Trade Receivables Discounting System (TReDS).  
- Export-oriented MSMEs are facing shipment delays, higher freight costs, and rising insurance premiums, tightening liquidity.  
- In volatile trade conditions, firms prefer regulated, bank-backed financing platforms like TReDS for steady cash flow and supplier continuity.  
- Cross-border invoice flows on receivables platforms are already moderating due to Gulf-region trade disruptions.  
- The UAE, particularly Jebel Ali Port, remains a vital logistics hub — with 10% of India’s merchandise exports heading there.  
- MSMEs contribute about 45% of India’s exports, underscoring their exposure to ongoing shipping bottlenecks.  
- Companies are increasingly turning to invoice factoring and credit lines to navigate payment delays and rising costs.  
- If disruptions persist, analysts expect greater reliance on TReDS for quick financing and improved working capital management.  

Key Takeaway: Trade route volatility is set to accelerate MSMEs’ adoption of TReDS platforms as a safer, faster liquidity lifeline during global supply chain stress.

> *India’s leading private space player targets next-gen computing infrastructure in orbit.*

- The company’s Vikram‑1 rocket launch is nearing completion after earlier technical delays.  
- Plans are underway to scale manufacturing capacity — shifting from one rocket per month to multiple launches per day over time.  
- Co-founder Pawan Kumar Chandana outlined a broader vision of developing rockets for future space infrastructure, including orbital computing capabilities.

🚨 Gas Supply Curbs Threaten Auto Production 
 
Tight LPG, PNG & Propane supplies begin to hit India’s automobile manufacturing chain.

- Tightening fuel availability is disrupting auto component production, hitting heat-intensive processes like casting, forging, and paint shops.  

- Government diversion of gas toward household LPG demand has cut supply to industries.  

- SIAM has urged authorities to restore gas allocations to auto OEMs and suppliers.  

- MSME component makers face operational strain; few have quick-switch options to alternative fuels.  

- ACMA warns that foundries and forging units are already affected, risking further supply disruptions.  

- Production delays may emerge if fuel shortages persist, impacting overall vehicle output and inventory levels.  

- Stable gaseous fuel supply remains critical for seamless auto manufacturing cycles.

Key Takeaway: Prolonged gas curbs could slow component output, delay vehicle deliveries, and hit the auto sector’s near-term momentum.

Bessembinder's Shocking Study

- ~75% lagged overall market index  
- Just 4% created ALL net wealth in stock market  

Indian Reality  
- Elite compounders like Asian Paints, Titan, HDFC Bank, TCS drive bulk returns  
- Thousands stagnate, underperform, or destroy capital  
- Most "slow growers" still beat market over decades  

Critical Investor Lesson  
Stock markets extremely unequal  
One great business held 10 years > dozens of mediocre stocks  

Key Takeaway:  
Hunt rare compounders—equal-weight portfolios guarantee mediocrity.

reports.text:
IEA Emergency Oil Release May Have Limited Impact

• 🛢️ Strategic Release: IEA plans 400 million barrels emergency oil release to offset Strait of Hormuz supply disruptions.

• ⏳ Short-Term Cover: Release could provide ~32–45 days of supply if deployed quickly.

• ⚙️ Logistics Challenge: Impact depends on timing, delivery speed, and distribution logistics.

• 🏭 Product Mix Issue: Stockpile includes both crude and refined products, not all immediately usable by refineries.

🟡 Impact: Neutral
Oil release may temporarily stabilize supply, but effectiveness depends on execution and logistics.

The Centre will release commercial LPG cylinders on priority to curb panic-driven bookings and restaurant shutdown risks amid the West Asia conflict, Petroleum Ministry Jt Secy Sujata Sharma said, also cautioning against panic bookings and fake news

🚆 QUADRANT FUTURE TEK: KAVACH SYSTEM ENTERS FINAL FIELD TRIALS

A key milestone in India’s railway safety modernization.

• 🚆 Passenger Field Trials: The company has entered the passenger train field trials stage for the KAVACH (Indian Railways Automatic Train Protection) system.

• 🧪 Highest Safety Compliance: The system has successfully cleared multi layer testing including SIL 4 certification, the highest safety standard for railway signalling systems.

• 📡 Next Key Trigger: After successful completion of field trials, the system is expected to receive RDSO certification, enabling wider deployment across Indian Railways.

• 📦 Strong Order Book: As of February 2026, the company has an executable order book of around ₹898 Cr.

• 🛠️ Large Execution Scope: Current contracts include KAVACH installation in 768 locomotives and coverage of 1,109 RKM of railway tracks.

🟢 Impact: Positive

Reaching the final trial stage significantly strengthens the visibility of commercial rollout for railway safety systems, positioning the company well for future railway modernization projects. 📈

reports.text: Mirae Asset Sharekhan Morning Tiger ( Pre - Market Insight ) - 12th March 2026.pdf

Why India Can't Ditch LPG for Electric Stoves Yet

Power Reality Check  
1.7 GW Data Centers (10 GW by 2030) could power 1 Cr kitchens — but locked 24/7 vs homes' 2-3 hr daily use.

Surplus Myth  
520 GW installed vs 250 GW peak demand — but grid can't deliver.

Root Cause: Last-Mile Crisis  
- Thin distribution lines/transformers melt under peak cooking load (200 GW if 10 Cr homes switch at dinner).  
- DISCOMs bankrupt (₹7L Cr debt), theft/subsidies/corruption kill efficiency.  
- "One Bulb" scarcity design since Independence.  

Peak Hour Nightmare  
10 Cr families x 2 KW = grid collapse 7-9 PM nationwide.

China vs India  
China: Infra ahead of demand — UHV lines, 99.9% reliability, LPG industrial-only.  
India: Demand-first, subsidy-driven — thin lines, perpetual shortages.

Key Takeaway  
India's energy poverty stems from last-mile grid failure despite surplus capacity — LPG stays essential until trillion-rupee upgrades or solar/smart grids arrive.

**LNG vs LPG Shortage Impact: Industry Breakdown**

**Core Differences**  
**LNG** (Liquefied Natural Gas): Methane-based, cryogenic (-162°C), used for power generation, industry, city gas.  
**LPG** (Liquefied Petroleum Gas): Refinery byproduct (propane/butane), cylinder-based, used for cooking, heating, autos.

**LNG Consumption Split (India)**  
- **Power**: ~45% (45 MMSCMD) – Gas plants face load cuts.  
- **Fertilizer**: ~25% (25 MMSCMD) – Urea production hit.  
- **City Gas (CGD)**: ~15% (15 MMSCMD) – CNG autos, PNG homes.  
- **Refineries/Petrochem**: ~10% (10 MMSCMD) – Feedstock squeeze.  
- **Others (Steel/Cement)**: ~5% (5 MMSCMD) – Industrial kilns.

**Industry Impacts**  
**Power**: Forced coal shift (+ve Coal India/NTPC); spot tariffs spike.  
**Fertilizer**: Subsidy burden up; urea imports surge.  
**CGD (IGL/MGL)**: CNG price hikes; PNG residential cutbacks.  
**Ceramics/Glass**: Kiln shutdowns (Somany hit earlier).  
**Hotels/Catering**: LPG pivot strained (just reported shortages).

**LPG Shortage Preview**  
Refinery cuts → cylinder queues → rural distress → organized players gain.

**Key Takeaway**  
LNG crunch hits power/fertilizer hardest (70% usage); LPG pinch squeezes hospitality/rural — coal/power stocks resilient amid energy pivot.

Right Issue Arbitrage: Real Opportunity or Trap?

Issue Snapshot  
Right issue at ₹10/share (vs CMP ₹115) in 1:1 ratio — headline arbitrage of ₹105/share (less fees/taxes).

Arbitrage Reality  
Yes, it's real and executable for eligible shareholders:  
- Apply at ₹10, get shares allotted.  
- Sell in open market at CMP for instant ₹105 gross gain/share.  
- Caveats: Renounce rights (if allowed) for premium; STT/CGT ~20-25% tax hit; liquidity risk if oversubscribed/delayed allotment.

- Company previously held significant stake in Kurlon Enterprises (mattress firm).  
- Sheela Foam acquired ~94.7% majority in 2023 (~₹2,035 Cr deal); recent buyback for remaining 5.34% at ₹588/share.  
- Current holding: Likely negligible/zero post-acquisition & buyback — Sheela Foam now controls Kurlon.

Key Takeaway  
₹105 arbitrage is genuine for rights holders (post-tax ~₹80 net), but verify company's balance sheet dilution risk — Kurlon stake now minimal.

China's Growth Mirage Exposed

Structural Headwinds  
Ruchir Sharma warns China's true long-term potential is just 2-3% amid depopulation, 340% debt-to-GDP, and collapsing productivity.

Desperate Stimulus  
Beijing fuels growth through over-investment and cut-price export flooding, gutting industries across Asia and Europe.

Breaking Model  
Credit efficiency collapsed — now requires $6 of new debt for every $1 of GDP growth, versus $1 two decades ago.

Key Takeaway  
China's debt-fueled export machine is cracking under demographic and productivity pressures, signaling sub-3% structural growth ahead.

PSBs Surge Ahead in Credit Growth Race

Loan Growth Leadership  
- Public sector banks achieve 17–28% YoY credit growth in FY26, outpacing private banks' 11–16%.  
- PSBs' loan market share rises to 54.4% (Dec 2025) from 53.1% YoY; private share slips to 40.6% from 41.1%.  

Top Performers  
- SBI, Canara Bank, Bank of Baroda, Union Bank post 18–28% advances growth, beating private peers.  

Growth Drivers  
- Momentum from improved asset quality, legacy bad loan recoveries, and corporate lending revival.  
- PSBs capture 50 bps market share in quarter, mainly from mid/small private banks.  

Profitability Gap  
- PSBs' combined net profit jumps 17.5% YoY to ₹55,000 crore.  
- Private banks manage only 3.2% YoY profit growth amid balance sheet adjustments.  

Key Takeaway:  
PSBs are reclaiming dominance through superior credit expansion and profitability, reshaping India's banking competitive landscape.

Strategic Channel Shift

- Reducing/restructuring offline sales teams and slashing store-related expenses.  
- Offline retail costs 20–24% of product price vs online's 8–10%, making e-commerce far more economical.  
- Outsourcing marketing and in-store promoters to third-party agencies to trim fixed costs.  

Market Risks  
- Heavy online reliance risks straining ties with traditional retailers and distributors.  
- Brands already hiking prices on sub-₹40,000 smartphones to offset component inflation.  

Supply Outlook  
- Industry estimates memory shortages persisting until 2027, keeping component prices elevated.  

Key Takeaway:  
Chinese smartphone majors' online pivot delivers cost efficiencies but tests offline partnerships amid prolonged memory supply constraints.

reports.text:
SEBI Income Surges 31% in FY25 Amid Audit Concerns

Financial Performance  
- SEBI reports total income of ₹2,713 crore in FY25, up 31% from ₹2,075 crore in FY24.  
- Capital fund (corpus) grows nearly 30% YoY to ₹7,235 crore.  

Revenue Drivers  
- Settlement and compounding charges jump sharply from ₹104 crore to ₹815 crore.  
- Investment portfolio expands with ₹2,857 crore in G-secs, ₹2,215 crore in bank deposits, and ₹30 crore in National Centre for Financial Education.  

CAG Audit Red Flags  
- Comptroller and Auditor General flags internal control lapses, including head office-branch account mismatches and unreconciled lease deposits.  
- Some disposed assets remain in books, highlighting accounting discrepancies.  

Key Takeaway:  
SEBI's robust FY25 financial growth underscores regulatory strength, but CAG's control concerns demand urgent accounting reforms.

SBI-MUFG Partnership Targets M&A, Aviation Financing

Strategic Collaboration  
- SBI partners with Japan's MUFG Bank to finance M&A, aviation, and real estate projects.  
- Aims to boost cross-border capital flows between India and Japan.  

Expanded Services  
- Joint efforts in M&A advisory, trade finance, and retail banking to aid Japanese firms entering India and Indian companies going global.  
- Leverages SBI's domestic strength and MUFG's global network for large-scale deals.  

SME Focus  
- Creates funding access for Indian mid-corporates and MSMEs via Japanese investors.  
- Follows RBI's recent M&A financing reforms.  

Proven Track Record  
- Builds on prior deals like $500M women's empowerment loan and MUFG's role in SBI's $1B social loan (2023).  

Key Takeaway:  
SBI-MUFG alliance unlocks massive financing opportunities, strengthening India-Japan economic ties across key growth sectors.

Ashok Leyland ₹500 Cr EV Battery Plant Boost

Major Investment Announced  
- Ashok Leyland to invest ₹400–500 crore in a greenfield EV battery pack plant at Pillaiyarpakkam, Tamil Nadu.  
- Facility will localize EV battery production, strengthening India’s electric mobility supply chain.  

Policy Alignment  
- Supports Tamil Nadu EV Policy 2023, National Mission on Transformative Mobility, and India’s 2070 net-zero goals.  
- Foundation stone laid by CM M.K. Stalin, positioning TN as EV manufacturing hub.  

Economic Impact  
- Expected to create jobs and skill development in the EV sector.  
- Part of broader strategy via Switch Mobility to expand electric commercial vehicle portfolio, per Dheeraj G. Hinduja.  

Key Takeaway:  
Ashok Leyland’s battery plant investment accelerates India’s EV ecosystem while cementing Tamil Nadu’s leadership in next-gen auto manufacturing.

Rasna Targets 20% Growth Amid Early Summer Surge

Seasonal Demand Boost  
- Rasna forecasts 20% revenue growth this summer as early heatwave drives beverage concentrate demand.  
- Holds 70% market share in concentrates, targeting 15–20% annual growth vs industry’s 10–12%.  

Strategic Expansion  
- Growth fueled by new product launches and 5x higher YouTube ad spend for digital push.  
- Expanding via quick-commerce, standalone stores, and e-commerce as key demand channels.  

Product Innovation  
- Reducing sugar content; some pre-mixes now sugar-free, requiring separate sugar addition.  
- Tapping micro/local markets with mass-market campaigns to broaden reach.  

Export Headwinds  
- West Asia conflict disrupts 40% of exports, causing shipment delays and container shortages.  
- Operates in 60+ countries with 12 manufacturing units across India.  

Key Takeaway:  
Early summer and digital expansion position Rasna for strong growth despite regional export challenges.

Gas Crisis Shifts Households to Electric Cooking

Supply Fears Drive Demand Surge  
- West Asia conflict sparks LPG and PNG supply fears, pushing households toward electric cooking alternatives.  
- Retailers report sharp spikes in induction cooktops, electric kettles, air fryers, and multi-use cookers across India.  

Retail Sales Explode  
- Croma sees induction cooktop sales nearly double, with daily run rates 3x normal levels.  
- Electric kettles and air fryers surge significantly amid precautionary buying.  

E-Commerce Boom  
- Flipkart and Amazon log massive search and sales spikes for electric appliances.  
- Amazon induction cooktop demand up 20x vs regular days; 30x sales growth in last 2 days alone.  
- Electric cooker sales rise 4x; quick-commerce like BigBasket reports 5x induction sales.  

Market Dynamics  
- Demand far exceeds normal seasonal trends, turning induction cooktops into "survival necessities".  
- Retailers warn of stock shortages in major cities if panic buying persists.  

Key Takeaway:  
Gas supply fears are rapidly accelerating India's shift to electric cooking, creating explosive demand and potential supply constraints for retailers.

AI Revolution Fuels Global Infrastructure Boom

Massive Investment Wave  
- Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang says AI is triggering the biggest infrastructure buildout in history, requiring trillions of dollars in global investment.  
- The surge spans data centres, chip fabs, and AI-focused infrastructure worldwide.  

Five-Layer AI Stack  
- Huang defines AI as a five-layer system: Energy → Chips → Infrastructure → Models → Applications — each driving exponential growth.  

Labour Surge Ahead  
- Expansion is spurring demand for skilled blue-collar workers — from electricians and steelworkers to plumbers and network techs.  
- Many of these high-paying technical roles are already in short supply, underscoring the massive workforce challenge ahead.  

Key Takeaway:  
AI is not just transforming software—it’s redefining the world’s physical infrastructure and labour economy on a trillion-dollar scale.

 Modis Navnirman Limited Receives Occupation Certificate for ‘Rashmi Celestia’ in Borivali West, Mumbai

The only way to end this war, is recognizing Iran’s legitimate rights, payment of reparations, and firm int'l guarantees against future aggression

 Iran President Masoud Pezeshkian
EnviroInfra Engineers shares gain 10% after winning order worth ₹411 crore in Bihar
IT services: The battle of narratives

SME Stocks Wealth Destruction: Key Lessons

Core Investing Principle  
- Extraordinary returns aren't essential – capital protection is paramount.  
- Wealth Protection First, Returns Later: Safe capital + patience = inevitable growth.  

Golden Rule  
- Never risk core capital chasing quick gains in speculative SME plays.  
- High-risk bets belong in satellite portfolios, not life savings.  

Practical Wisdom  
- Protect Capital → Stay Patient → Wealth Will Follow.  
- Time rewards disciplined investors; greed destroys impatient ones.  

Key Takeaway:  
SME wealth destruction teaches that surviving market cycles with intact capital guarantees long-term success over chasing lottery-ticket returns.

Promoter Buying Signals Confidence

Time Technoplast  
- Promoters actively increasing stake through open market purchases.  
- Recent buying shows strong management conviction in growth prospects.  

Aarti Pharmalabs  
- Promoter stake accumulation signals faith in pharma business trajectory.  
- Insider buying typically precedes positive operational developments.  

Apollo Pipes  
- Management backing company with personal capital deployment.  
- Promoter purchases highlight undervaluation perception at current levels.  

Investor Signal  
- All three stocks show active promoter buying – classic bullish indicator.  
- Management putting "skin in the game" suggests attractive risk-reward.  

Key Takeaway:  
Time Technoplast, Aarti Pharmalabs, and Apollo Pipes promoter buying reflects strong insider confidence – watch these names closely for potential upside.

Fuel Crisis Cripples Gig Drivers Amid Geopolitical Tensions

Supply Disruptions Hit Hard  
- Ola, Uber, and Rapido drivers face LPG and CNG shortages due to geopolitical tensions impacting fuel supply chains.  
- Long queues at fuel stations with 2–2.5 hour waits and lines stretching up to 1 km in major cities.  

Cost Surge in Key Markets  
- In Hyderabad, LPG prices for auto drivers jumped from ₹65.70/kg to ₹92.97/kg, sharply raising operating costs.  
- Fewer refills mean fewer rides completed daily, directly slashing driver earnings.  

Company Response  
- Ride-hailing platforms are monitoring closely and engaging government authorities to reduce disruptions for drivers and customers.  

Key Takeaway:  
Fuel shortages and price hikes threaten gig economy stability, forcing drivers into survival mode with eroding incomes and reduced ride supply.

Aster DM–Quality Care Merger to Create India’s 2nd Largest Hospital Chain

Major Expansion Ahead  
- Aster DM Healthcare to add 4,000+ beds over the next 3 years post-merger with Quality Care India Ltd (QCIL), backed by Blackstone and TPG.  
- Combined entity to operate 10,625 beds across 39 hospitals in 9 states, ranking No. 2 in India by bed capacity.

Future Growth Plans  
- Targeting 14,710 beds by FY28, with majority additions from Aster and the rest from QCIL.  
- Committed ₹2,300 crore capex for expansion; ₹350 crore already deployed in key ongoing projects.

Regulatory Milestones Cleared  
- Approvals received from CCI, BSE, NSE, and shareholders.  
- NCLT approval expected shortly to finalize the merger.

Profitability Outlook  
- Integration expected to enhance operational efficiency.  
- Combined entity aims for ~20% EBITDA margins post full synergy realization.

Key Takeaway:  
The Aster–QCIL merger positions the group as a major healthcare powerhouse with aggressive expansion, strong backing, and improving margins.

PRESS NOTE 3 EASING: ELECTRONICS BOOST  

Investment Unlock  
Press Note 3 relaxation speeds minority FDI + tech tie-ups in components — critical for localization.  

Scheme Synergy  
₹40,000 Cr ECMS targets passives, PCBs, semiconductor materials production.  

Impact Drivers  
- Attracts global tech partners.  
- Accelerates Indian OEM collaborations.  
- Clears ownership hurdles for faster project execution.  

Context  
India excels in assembly but imports components — policy fills supply chain gap.  

Takeaway  
FDI easing + ECMS = faster electronics self-reliance; watch smartphone/local OEM partnerships.

AARTI INDUSTRIES: $150MN AGROCHEM CONTRACT  

Major Win  
Secures USD 150 Mn (~₹1,385 Cr) supply deal with global agrochem giant.  

Contract Details  
- Tenure: Valid through Mar 2030 (medium-term).  
- Scope: Specialty chemicals/agrochem intermediates supply.  

Strategic Boost  
Multi-year order locks revenue visibility + lifts capacity utilization.  

Takeaway  
Long-term export contract de-risks growth amid volatile chemical cycles.

PAINT PRICES: 2–5% APRIL HIKE LIKELY  

Crude Trigger  
West Asia war/Hormuz disruptions keep oil elevated — paints face 2–5% rise in Apr if sustained.  

Cost Exposure  
- Crude inputs (TiO2, solvents, binders, resins): 55–60% of costs.  
- $1/bbl oil uptick erodes EBITDA ~25 bps sans pass-through.  

Inventory Buffer  
- 30–45 days stock delays immediate hikes.  

Margin Outlook FY26  
- Asian Paints: 18–20%.  
- Berger Paints: 15–17%.  
- Kansai Nerolac: 13–15%.  

Takeaway  
Oil volatility tests margin resilience — watch conflict duration for pricing power.

**CLSA: BHARTI AIRTEL DEBT-FREE CATALYST**  

**Rating & Target**  
- **Outperform** | **Target Price: ₹2310**  

**Rights Issue Final Call**  
- **₹158bn final call** on **₹210bn 2021 equity rights issue**.  
- Issued at **₹535/share** (~10% discount then; ~70% discount today).  
- Resulted in **7% equity dilution**.  

**Stakeholder Dynamics**  
- **2025 promoter stake sale**: ~4ppt reduction.  
- **Singtel effective stake**: **27.5%** vs **Sunil Mittal 21.4%**.  
- **6% stock overhang** from stake equalization expectations.  
- **Rising FII/DII ownership** amid overhang.  

**FCF & Balance Sheet**  
- **9MFY26 consol. FCF**: **₹478bn ($5.5bn)**.  
- Proceeds enable **bank-debt-free** status.  

**Key Takeaway:**  
Airtel's rights call + stellar FCF clears debt overhang — rising institutional interest supports re-rating to ₹2310.

- **Critical intermediate dispatches** start seamlessly from **Mar'26**.  

**Contract Safeguards**

**Distribution Push**

- Planned **entry into southern markets**.  

**Domestic Outlook**

**@Stockupdate9**

**GOLDMAN SACHS: OIL PRICES GETTING RISK PREMIUM**  

**Supply Shock Scenario**  
- **Strait of Hormuz disruption**: Assumed lasting **21 days**.  
- **Persian Gulf exports hit**: **16.2 million barrels per day**.  

**Revised Forecasts**  
- **Brent Q4 2026**: Raised to **$71** per barrel.  
- **WTI Q4 2026**: Raised to **$67** per barrel.  
- **March trajectory**: Prices likely **rise through uncertainty**.  

**Market Response**  
- **Large risk premium** required for supply shock.  
- **Global SPR releases**: Could reach **254 million barrels**.  
- **Policy mitigation**: May halve inventory impact.  

**Upside Bias**  
- **Price risks skewed higher** if disruptions persist.  

**Key Takeaway:**  
Hormuz supply shock builds risk premium into oil — persistent disruption sends Brent/WTI meaningfully higher despite SPR counterbalance.

**@Stockupdate9**

  - **Road congestion** from 3W surge.  
  - **Pollution rise** (CNG rickshaws emit PM10/NOx via idling).  
  - **"Fare drought"** — oversupply erodes driver earnings.  

**Market Impact Assessment**

- Proposed cap: **1.4mn permits** — supports **steady replacement demand**.  
- Demand levels expected to **remain intact**.  

**Bajaj Auto Positioning**  
- **Non-material event** — limited P&L impact.

  - **Disappointing Q3 performance**.

- **Retail demand healthy** across **cars and UVs** — end-customer strength intact.  
- **Wholesale capped by capacity constraints** — to ease from **Apr'26** with new capacity online.  

**Earnings Outlook**  
- Expect **16% earnings CAGR** over **FY25–28E**.  

**Key Takeaway:**  
Maruti's capacity unlock from Apr'26 resolves wholesale bottleneck — healthy retail + volume ramp supports re-rating to MOSL's ₹17,406 target.

**CLSA: IRAN CONFLICT RISKS INDIA'S ENERGY SUPPLY**  

**Vulnerability Hierarchy**  
- **LPG most vulnerable**, followed by **LNG**.  
- Alternate supply support arrives only **end-April**.  
- **Acute shortages** likely over **next 3–4 weeks**.  

**Sector Output Impacts**  
- **Fertilisers, petrochemicals**: Production disruptions expected.  
- **Restaurants, glass, ceramics**: Significant capacity hits.  
- **Petrol & diesel**: Lower risk as India remains **net exporter**.  

**Corporate Pressure Points**  
- **OMCs**: IOC, BPCL, HPCL face **margin compression**.  
- **Gas utilities**: GAIL, IGL, MGL, Guj Gas, Petronet LNG under strain.  

**Key Takeaway:**  
LPG/LNG supply crunch threatens key industries short-term — OMCs/gas utilities vulnerable, but petrol/diesel exports provide defensive buffer.

- **Integrated "follow-the-molecule" model** drives end-to-end value capture.

- **Presence in high-margin oligos** segment.  

**Execution Strengths**  
- **Ample headroom for growth** across capacity and client base.  
- **Strong win rates** in competitive project bids.  
- **Established global presence** with diversified revenue streams.  

**Financial Health**  
- **Clean balance sheet** supports aggressive expansion without dilution risks.  
- **No PE supply overhang** — promoter-aligned ownership structure.  
- **Valuations align with peers** despite superior growth trajectory.  

**Target Revision**  
- **FY28 sales/EPS estimates raised** by **3%****/5****%**.  
- **Maintain Buy** | **Revised PT: ₹1,300**.  

**Key Takeaway:**  
Sai Life combines structural tailwinds, execution excellence, and clean financials — positioning it as the premier CRDMO compounder for the next cycle.

@Stockupdate9
@Fundamental3

**UBS: TITAN'S COMPOUNDING STORY INTACT**  

**Rating & Target**  
- **Buy** | **Target Price:** **₹5,300**  

**Demand Strength**  
- **Store feedback confirms very strong jewellery demand** across key segments.  
- **Growth momentum sustained**, despite **Middle East disruptions** potentially weighing on international expansion.  

**Strategic Positioning**  
- **Titan midway through its compounding journey** with significant growth runway ahead.  
- **Consistent operating performance and margin delivery** continue to **exceed expectations**.  
- **Prime beneficiary** of the **long-term shift** from unorganised to organised jewellery sector.  

**Key Takeaway:**  
Titan's execution excellence and formalisation tailwinds position it for sustained market share gains and superior returns.

**JEFFERIES: BHARTI AIRTEL STAYS ATTRACTIVE**  

**Rating & Target**  
- **Buy** | **Target Price:** **₹2,250** (cut from ₹2,575)  

**Earnings Adjustments**  
- **India revenue/EBITDA estimates cut 6–8%** due to potential **delays in Jio's IPO**.  
- **Target multiple lowered** from **13x to 12x** amid **NBFC foray raising capital allocation concerns**.  

**Risk Factors**  
- **Rising inflation risk** from **higher energy prices** impacting operating costs.  

**Bull Case**  
- **14% EBITDA growth** expected, with **limited energy price exposure**.  
- **Attractive risk-reward** supports the **Buy** recommendation.  

**Key Takeaway:**  
Despite near-term headwinds from delayed tariff hikes and diversification risks, Bharti's growth trajectory and valuation make it a compelling defensive pick.

@Concalls3

**JEFFERIES: MIDEAST CONFLICT IMPACT ON FINANCIALS**  

**Divergent Sector Exposure**  
- **Banks**: Expect **loan growth acceleration**, but monitor **higher credit costs** as key risk.  
- **NBFCs**: Likely to see **NIM compression**, **elevated credit costs**, and **slower growth**.  
- **Vulnerable segments**: SME (EXIM/gas-linked), **CV loans**, and **overseas exposures** face heightened risk.  

**Valuation Buffer**  
- Lender valuations trading **near lows**, suggesting **limited downside potential**.  
- **Exchanges** to benefit from **rising market volatility**.  

**Lower Impact Segments**  
- **Life/General Insurance** and **Fintech** face **lesser risk**.  
- **Capital market players** more exposed to **disruption effects**.  

**Key Takeaway:**  
While Middle East tensions create selective credit pressures, attractive valuations and volatility tailwinds limit systemic downside for Indian financials.

@Stocksip

**MORGAN STANLEY: DIXON FACES PLI HEADWINDS**  

**Rating & Target**  
- **Underweight** | **Target Price:** **₹8,157**  

**PLI Impact Assessment**  
- Assume Dixon books **₹220 Cr PLI** in mobile segment for FY26 — representing **17% of EBITDA** and **40 bps margin contribution**.  
- **PLI allocation to mobile** revised **lower by 22%** in FY26; FY27 budget estimate at just **₹1,350 Cr**.  

**Uncertainty Factors**  
- Reports suggest **PLI 2.0 possibility**, but government direction remains **unclear**.  
- **Current DRAM prices** reduce **visibility on Q4/FY27 mobile shipments**.  

**Key Takeaway:**  
PLI scheme revisions and component pricing pressures cloud Dixon's near-term margin outlook, warranting caution despite electronics growth potential.

@Stocksip

**MACQUARIE: ONGC'S PRODUCTION RAMP-UP KEY**  

**Rating & Target**  
- **Outperform** | **Target Price:** **₹300** (down from ₹310)  

**Core Thesis**  
- **Current price volatility** viewed as **positive entry opportunity**.  
- **Sustained production growth** remains **critical for re-rating**.  
- **2025** marked **stability year** — **de-growth arrested** successfully.  
- Expect **significant production ramp-up** through **2026**.  

**Earnings & Valuation**  
- **FY26/27 EPS cut** by **23.8%****/15****.4%** due to lower-than-expected production outlook.  
- **Attractive dividend yield** offers **possible upside potential**.  

**Key Takeaway:**  
ONGC's re-rating hinges on 2026 production execution — current volatility presents a compelling risk-reward setup for patient investors.

@Stocksip

**UBS: TITAN STILL IN THE SWEET SPOT**  

**Rating & Target**  
- **Buy** | **Target Price:** ₹5,300  

**Key Observations**  
- **Jewellery demand remains very strong**, supported by sustained festive and wedding purchases.  
- **Growth momentum intact**, though **Middle East disruptions** could modestly impact international operations.  
- **Titan remains mid-way through its compounding journey**, with ample runway for expansion in core and emerging categories.  
- **Operating performance and margins** continue to **exceed expectations**, reflecting disciplined execution.  
- Seen as a **key beneficiary of the structural shift** from the **unorganised to the organised jewellery market**.  

**Key Takeaway:**  
UBS stays bullish — Titan’s strong brand, execution consistency, and market formalisation tailwinds reinforce its long-term compounding story.

@Stocksip

**UBS INDIA STRATEGY: BULLISH STANCE MAINTAINED**  

**Portfolio Changes**  
- **Removed:** HPCL and BHEL.  
- **Increased Allocation:** **InterGlobe Aviation**, **Larsen & Toubro**, and **Thermax** — by **1 ppt each**.  

**Market View**  
- UBS maintains an **“Attractive” rating** on Indian equities.  
- Sees **robust macro fundamentals** and **proactive policy support** sustaining growth momentum.  

**Policy & Growth Drivers**  
- Government has **front-loaded growth measures** — including **income and GST tax cuts**, along with **labor law simplification**.  
- **RBI** has **cut rates** and **injected liquidity**, ensuring policy coordination to support expansion.  
- These steps are expected to **keep GDP growth near trend**, with **FY26 growth seen at 7.4%**.  

**Earnings Outlook**  
- **Nifty EPS growth:** projected at **single-digit** for **FY26**, rising to **double-digit** in **FY27–FY28**.  
- Medium-term view remains **constructive**, led by **ongoing reforms**, **resilient domestic demand**, and **steady corporate earnings growth**.  

**Key Takeaway:**  
UBS sees India as a structural outperformer in Asia — policy-led growth support, reform momentum, and earnings recovery underpin its positive outlook.

**NOMURA: BULLISH ON NBFC TRANSFORMATION STORY**  

**Fresh Coverage Initiation**  
- **Piramal Finance** – *Buy*, Target **₹2,150**  
- **L&T Finance** – *Buy*, Target **₹325**  
- **Tata Capital** – *Buy*, Target **₹400**  
- **HDB Finance** – *Neutral*, Target **₹760**  

**Growth Outlook**  
- Nomura expects all four NBFCs to post **high-teens to 20%+ loan growth CAGR** over **FY26–28**.  
- **Three of the four** to see **ROE expansion beyond mid-teens** by FY28 as profitability strengthens.  
- Sector theme: **"Diversified lenders – Everyone, everywhere, all the time."**  

**Stock-Specific Views**  
- **Tata Capital** – Strong loan growth trajectory with ample headroom for **profitability expansion**.  
- **Piramal Finance & L&T Finance** – Undergoing **structural transformations**; both seen as **long-term compounders** with meaningful **upside potential**.  
- **HDB Finance** – Needs **rebound in loan growth** momentum for a **re-rating catalyst**.  

**Sector Insight**  
- Nomura highlights Indian regulators’ **preparation for AI adoption** across lending ecosystems, supporting digital scalability and prudent risk management.  

**Key Takeaway:**  
Nomura sees India’s leading diversified NBFCs entering a new growth cycle – led by digital transformation, improving ROEs, and strong balance-sheet discipline.

**UBS on Bajaj Auto: Maharashtra 3W Permit Freeze**

**Policy Impact**  
- Maharashtra govt **halts new auto-rickshaw permits** to curb congestion/pollution.  
- Targets **traffic management** in key urban centers.

**Bajaj Exposure**  
- **~87% market share** in Maharashtra **ICE 3W** segment.  
- State contributes **17%** of domestic ICE 3W sales (ex-Telangana).

**Margin Vulnerability**  
- **ICE 3Ws** generate **>30% margins**—high sensitivity to volume slowdown.  
- Policy directly crimps **new registrations**.

**Risk Assessment**  
- **Negative near-term** on volumes/margins.  
- EV transition may mitigate long-term, but ICE dominance hurts short.

**Key Takeaway**  
**Permit freeze** pressures Bajaj's high-margin 3W engine—watch for EV pivot acceleration.

Portfolio additions & deletions by Nemish Shah, Ashish Kacholia, Mukul Agrawal, Anil Kumar Goel, Rakesh Jhunjhunwala, Ashish Dhawan, Hemendra Kothari, Vijay Kedia, Madhusudan Kela, Akash Bhanshali, etc as of December 2025 quarter

Every time since 2001 that the benchmark index has stayed flat for ~17 months, a strong rally has followed.

History shows ~30% average gains in the next 12 months & ~76% over the following 3 yrs.

This is the 14th such instance — and investors are watching closely. 

C: ETMarket

IndiaMART is suing OpenAI.

IndiaMART discovered last October that ChatGPT has been completely ignoring it while competitor platforms like TradeIndia continue to appear normally. 

The US Trade Representative (USTR) has flagged IndiaMART as a "notorious market" for counterfeits and OpenAI, wary of liability, simply stopped surfacing it.

IndiaMART filed a petition in the Calcutta High Court on 27 December, arguing selective discrimination. OpenAI's defense is that it has no office or permanent establishment in India.

AI is the new search layer and India has no framework recognizing a "right to be surfaced" in an AI response.

Nomura Refiners

Believe refineries such as Reliance, IOC, BPCL & HCPL will likely have to partially switch to other sources of fuel such as fuel oil to replace 35% of potential volume curtailment

Fuel oil being cheaper than imported gas may not have any negative earnings impact for these companies, though there could be short-term environmental impact, especially in some of critically-polluted areas

Nomura GAIL

Expect operational impact on GAIL’s Pata petchem plant which relies completely on gas sourced from Qatar Energy

However, financial impact may not be material given already poor economics of this plant due to soft petchem spreads

GAIL will likely benefit from 100% gas allocation to the LPG production plant (Priority I segment), though the overall impact could be minimal due to a small contribution of LPG production to overall earnings (4% of consolidated EBITDA in FY26F)

Gas transmission and marketing businesses contribute bulk of GAIL’s earnings (~89% to EBITDA in FY26F)

CLSA on ASK Auto

O-P, TP Rs 630

Recent stock correction appears driven largely by relative underperformance of small-cap stocks in India over past three months, & believe reaction is overdone

ASK delivered robust YoY revenue/Ebitda growth of 18%/27%, in third quarter, with its strong execution outlook intact—supported by healthy retail 2W volume run-rates

At CMP, ASK trades at c.17x FY28CL earnings despite delivering a 28% ROCE & EPS Cagr of 20% over FY26-28CL, & faces no business risk from raw material inflation given full pass-through to customers.

JPM on Life Insurance

Initiate OW on Max fin – TP Rs 2170

India’s life insurance sector is turning the corner with GST exemption driving a strong uptick in retail protection.

Industry discussion around a rejig of payouts is likely to benefit customers and insurers as long-term customer proposition improves.

Margins, now at trough levels, look set to recover as protection sales rise and payout structures normalize.

Given sharp stock price declines ; rate SBI Life, HDFC Life, IPRU and LIC at Overweight

Key catalysts: commission reform, sustained protection growth, margin recovery, and share gains by larger insurers.

@Beatthestreetnews

What happens if oil supply from the Persian Gulf is disrupted?

Goldman Sachs models different disruption scenarios:

• 10 day disruption: Brent fair value ~$66
• 21 day disruption: Brent ~$71
• 30 day disruption: Brent $93**

Key insight:
Oil markets react sharply to supply shocks from the Persian Gulf because it accounts for a large share of global crude flows.

Another important dynamic:

• Every $10 increase in Brent can destroy roughly 0.5 to 1.8 mb/d of demand depending on price levels.

Meaning:
Higher oil prices eventually reduce demand, creating a natural stabilizing mechanism for the oil market.

India built strategic buffers for oil. But for gas, we are dangerously exposed.

India holds strategic crude reserves, yet LNG storage covers barely 21 days of inventory, even as gas demand rises and supply routes through West Asia face disruption.

With India importing 85% of its crude and 50% of its gas, any prolonged conflict around Hormuz can quickly tighten LNG supply and spike prices.

Countries like China, Japan and Korea mandate 10% gas storage of annual demand. India has none.

In the next energy shock, the real vulnerability may not be oil. But it could be natural gas.

India may build 8 to 10 GW of data center capacity by 2030 :)

For every 1 GW data center you roughly need 4,000 to 8,000 km cables, about 25,000 to 40,000 tons copper, and the cable cost alone is around ₹3500 to ₹4000 crore per GW.

So if India builds 10 GW, that is roughly a ₹35,000 to ₹40,000 crore cable opportunity.

From this, the Data center segment alone may generate around ₹2500 to ₹3000 crore revenue, and the industry may grow 20 percent plus CAGR

Morgan Stanley on India Energy

Impact of oil shocks in India has varied since price deregulation was introduced in 2014

With oil equities typically underperforming initially but then significantly outperforming in the two years after a shock

See 2026 shaping up for a similar response, and prefer Indian refiners and OMCs

As tends to happen in energy shocks, governments freeze fuel prices

2026 will likely be no different

We think policy stability will be maintained, with no windfall taxes (considering the stability laws passed by parliament in 2025)

Continue to view Indian refiners and OMCs positively and would look to add during underperformance

Indian refiners and gas users are set to play an essential role in reducing supply disruptions in fuel products as they absorb the US natural gas glut in the months ahead

Approx +5% rise in fuel cost could impact EPS by estimate 5-7%

Househelp Apps Cross 2 Million Monthly Orders but Profitability Concerns Grow

India’s instant househelp platforms like Urban Company, Snabbit, and Pronto have crossed 2 million monthly orders in February, up 45–50% from about 1.3 million in December.

Urban Company leads with 840,000–850,000 orders, followed by Snabbit (~830,000) and Pronto (~340,000).

Market share in February:
Urban Company: 49.2%
Snabbit: 36.4%
Pronto: 14.3%

Companies are expanding aggressively into new cities and micro-markets, increasing scale in the 10-minute househelp services segment.

To acquire users, platforms are offering deep discounts, with services priced as low as ₹1 per hour in some markets.

The sector is burning significant cash, with combined spending of about $10–11 million in February.

Investors are increasingly questioning whether the business model can achieve sustainable profitability.

Urban Company’s stock has fallen over 40% since listing, reflecting concerns over unit economics.
